content: The biologically-inspired working memory retention gating mechanism from cognitive neuroscience can be adapted to create a dynamic gating module for Intrinsic Curiosity Module (ICM) in large-scale autonomous agents, where only unexpected, prediction-error-coded novelty features that meet a long-term retention threshold are stored instead of caching all forward and inverse model embeddings. This targeted retention gating cuts memory footprint by only persisting high-value curiosity-driven learning signals that generalize to future unexplored states, rather than discarding all embeddings or storing redundant transient novelty.
source_topics: Memory-efficient ICM implementation for large-scale autonomous agents
trigger_topic: Memory-efficient ICM implementation for large-scale autonomous agents
